steps.title: Set up the earnings equation
steps.content: Let $x$ be hours at job A and $y$ be hours at job B. Avery earns \$10 per hour at job A and \$20 per hour at job B, so the graph represents $$10x+20y=s.$$

steps.title: Use the intercept instead of doing extra algebra
steps.content: The line crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,8)$. That point means Avery works $0$ hours at job A and $8$ hours at job B. Substitute just that point into the earnings equation: $$10(0)+20(8)=s.$$ So $$s=160.$$

steps.title: Match the equation to the choices
steps.content: The equation with $s=160$ is $10x+20y=160$. Its $y$-intercept is $8$ because setting $x=0$ gives $20y=160$, so $y=8$. That matches B.
